questions.question: Consider the following predicate logic statement: ∀x (Person(x) → Human(x)). What is the meaning of this statement in natural language?
questions.hint: The statement means that all people are human.

questions.question: Represent the following knowledge in predicate logic: 'All humans are mortal and all mortals are humans.'
questions.hint: Use the following predicates: Human(x), Mortal(x), and ∀∀∃∀.

questions.question: Consider the predicate logic statement: ∃x (Person(x) ∧ Human(x) ∧ Age(x) > 18). What does this statement mean in natural language?
questions.hint: The statement means that there exists at least one person who is a human and older than 18 years old.
